defs: Pain; especially, pain of travail; throe.
Time; while; space of time; moment; trice.
quotes: I will with Thomas speak a little throw.

records.ety: [OE. þrowen, þrawen, to throw, to twist, AS. þrāwan to twist, to whirl; akin to D. draaijen, G. drehen, OHG. drājan, L. terebra an auger, gimlet, Gr. <?/ to bore, to turn, <?/ to pierce, <?/ a hole. Cf. Thread, Trite, Turn, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. Threw (thr); p. p. Thrown (thrōn); p. pr. & vb. n. Throwing.]

defs: To fling, cast, or hurl with a certain whirling motion of the arm, to throw a ball; -- distinguished from to toss, or to bowl.

To fling or cast in any manner; to drive to a distance from the hand or from an engine; to propel; to send; as, to throw stones or dust with the hand; a cannon throws a ball; a fire engine throws a stream of water to extinguish flames.
To drive by violence; as, a vessel or sailors may be thrown upon a rock.
To cause to take a strategic position; as, he threw a detachment of his army across the river.
To overturn; to prostrate in wrestling; as, a man throws his antagonist.
To cast, as dice; to venture at dice.
To put on hastily; to spread carelessly.
quotes: O'er his fair limbs a flowery vest he threw.

defs: To divest or strip one's self of; to put off.
quotes: There the snake throws her enameled skin.

defs: To form or shape roughly on a throwing engine, or potter's wheel, as earthen vessels.
To give forcible utterance to; to cast; to vent.
quotes: I have thrown A brave defiance in King Henry's teeth.

defs: To bring forth; to produce, as young; to bear; -- said especially of rabbits.

To twist two or more filaments of, as silk, so as to form one thread; to twist together, as singles, in a direction contrary to the twist of the singles themselves; -- sometimes applied to the whole class of operations by which silk is prepared for the weaver.
To perform the act of throwing or casting; to cast; specifically, to cast dice.
The act of hurling or flinging; a driving or propelling from the hand or an engine; a cast.
quotes: He heaved a stone, and, rising to the throw, He sent it in a whirlwind at the foe.

defs: A stroke; a blow.
quotes: Nor shield defend the thunder of his throws.

defs: The distance which a missile is, or may be, thrown; as, a stone's throw.
A cast of dice; the manner in which dice fall when cast; as, a good throw.
An effort; a violent sally.
quotes: Your youth admires The throws and swellings of a Roman soul.

defs: The extreme movement given to a sliding or vibrating reciprocating piece by a cam, crank, eccentric, or the like; travel; stroke; as, the throw of a slide valve. Also, frequently, the length of the radius of a crank, or the eccentricity of an eccentric; as, the throw of the crank of a steam engine is equal to half the stroke of the piston.
A potter's wheel or table; a jigger. See 2d Jigger, 2 (a).
A turner's lathe; a throwe.
The amount of vertical displacement produced by a fault; -- according to the direction it is designated as an upthrow, or a downthrow.
